title: JavaScript Source
documentation:
## Purpose
JavaScript source files contain code written in ECMAScript, the scripting language that powers web browser interactivity and Node.js server-side applications. JavaScript is the dominant language of the web and a common choice for full-stack development.

## When to use
Use [JavaScript Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:javascript;textable;code) for `.js`, `.mjs`, and `.cjs` files. The `.mjs` extension signals ES module format (using `import`/`export`), while `.cjs` signals CommonJS format (using `require`). For React JSX files, use [JavaScript JSX Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:jsx;textable;code) instead.

## Notes
JavaScript is dynamically typed and interpreted; no compilation step is required for execution in a browser or Node.js environment, though bundlers (Webpack, Rollup, esbuild) and transpilers (Babel) are common in production workflows. TypeScript files (`.ts`) are distinct — see [TypeScript Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:typescript;textable;code).
